text:
Market today: Mixed/cautious tone. The Morgan Stanley chip warning is the real driver for your book — Wilson is drawing comparisons to silver's boom-bust cycle, which hits directly at your chip-heavy, margin-leveraged portfolio.

Your holdings: AVGO is the one screaming for attention — your largest position, down 15.45%, and now chip sentiment is getting called out specifically by a major bank. That's a double hit. SPAL is down 14.64% and it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily — that's a ticking clock, not a hold. PTNM is down 15.25% with almost no liquidity data — tiny spec on margin is dangerous. All three: margin amplifies every one of these drops.

Watch: 1) AVGO price action vs. your $336 stop — do not let emotion override it. 2) SpaceX Nasdaq-100 inclusion news — direct tailwind for SPCX (brand-new, very volatile IPO, manage manually).

Bottom line: Your biggest risk today is letting three simultaneous double-digit losers ride on margin while chip sentiment turns bearish — know exactly where you cut.
